calendar.matters:
ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Feb-28-2019 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Baron Miller for and with Timothy Breen (Petitioner/Administrator).
Ted Broomfield for and with Raymond Choy (Beneficiary/Objector).

Mr. Broomfield stated that the supplement is self evident of the statutory fees. Mr. Miller stated that he was forced to file petitions, that he is entitled to be paid for his work/extraordinary fees, that they requested a reserve because they needed to file tax returns, spoke of Mr. Choy's actions in the past and stated that he does know that a petition for final distribution will be an end to this. Mr. Miller also stated that he does not know if Mr. Choy will be filing other motions or appeals. The First and Final Account, etc. is under submission.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Chan. Not reported.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Jan-28-2019 Continued To Feb-25-19 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Baron Miller for and with Timothy Breen (Administrator).
Ted Broomfield for and with Raymond Choy (Beneficiary/Objector).

The Court informed Mr. Miller that when it is being asked to approve extraordinary fees, it needs time sheets for the ordinary services to make sure there is no duplication of time. The Court also informed Counsel that it was not provided with copies of the objections and that courtesy copies of all filed documents are needed. The First and Final Account, etc. is continued to February 25, 2019 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Chan. Not reported.

Petition Requesting Sanction Against Administrator Timothy P. Breen And His Attorney Baron L. Miller For Failure To Fulfill Their Fiduciary Duty To Get Court Confirmation Of A Monafide Offer Of $1.75 Million For 1/2 Interest Of 1360-1370 Pine St And Interfere With Our Close Of Escrow In March 14, 2017
Off Calendar May-29-2018 Denied
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Baron Miller for and with Timothy Breen (Administrator)

The Court noted the petitioner Mr. Raymond Choy is not present, and inquired if Mr. Miller has heard from Mr. Choy. Mr. Miller stated that he has not heard from Mr. Choy. The Petition Requesting Sanction against Administrator and His Attorney for Failure to Fulfill their Fiduciary Duty, etc. is DENIED. Mr. Miller is to submit a proposed order, approved as to form.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Shirley Le. Deputy: V. Jung. Not reported.

Petition By Administrator To Determine Ownership Of Estate Assets And For Order Directing Transfer Of Those Assets
Off Calendar Mar-01-2018 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Baron Miller, for and with petitioner Timothy Breen; Raymond Choy, trustee in pro per; Richard Traverso.

Mr. Choy stated that he was objecting to the petitin filed by Mr. Miller. Mr. Miller responded that he had reviewed the proposed submission from Mr. Choy, and said that it did not raise any objection to the petition. He reported that Mr. and Mrs. Choy had sold a piece of real property years ago and had then refused to complete the sale. The buyer had been forced to sue the sellers. The Choys had appealed a final order for sale of the property, and then Mrs. Choy had died. Ultimately the property sale closed using the buyer's attorney as escrow agent. There is now a balance of $100,000 in sales proceeds being held by the title company from the sale. Mr. Choy is entitled to $75,000 of this sum, with the remainder representing the interest of his wife's estate, but Mr. Choy refuses to consent to the distribution of the $25,000 to his wife's estate. Mr. Breen as sdministrator of Mrs. Choy's estate is seeking to obtain an order for payment of the $25,000 to Mrs. Choy's estate. Mr. Choy replied that Mr. Breen and Mr. Miller have refused to pay any of the proceeds of sale to the seller, and the funds are bening held for payment of the administrator's attorneys fees. Mr. Breen stated that the estate has no assets other than its sahre of the escrowed funds. Mr. Miller stated that there is a $462,500.00 bond on file, and that he has made no demand in the escrow for payment of attorney's fees. The matter was taken under submission.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Craig Blackstone. Bailiff: Deputy V. Jung. (Not reported)

Petition For Order Instructing Administrator To Convey Title To Real Property
Off Calendar Feb-17-2017 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Baron Miller for Timothy Breen.
Howard Olsen for and with Raymond Choy (Objector).

The Court noted that objections have now been filed. Mr. Miller stated that all he saw and was served with was a declaration, and that he gets the impression Mr. Choy wants to continue this matter until after the civil matter is heard by Judge Wong. Mr. Olsen stated that there should be a concern about the Administrator's continuing duties. The Court granted the Petition for Order Instructing Administrator to Convey Title to Real Property (subject to review of the order). The Court informed Counsel that it will add language to the order that requires the Administrator to proceed pursuant to terms of the sale contract and consistent with judgment performance.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: A. Jaime. Not reported.

Petition For Order Instructing Administrator To Convey Title To Real Property
Off Calendar Jan-24-2017 Continued To Feb-07-17 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Baron Miller for and with Timothy Breen (Administrator).
Howard Olsen for and with Todd Choy and Raymond Choy (Objectors).

Mr. Olsen stated that he filed a declaration yesterday regarding a continuance, that he got the file yesterday and that he is aware there is other litigation. Mr. Olsen also requested two to three weeks to assess what he can and cannot do, and stated that he would be obtaining something to reverse the appointment of Mr. Breen and re-appointing Todd Choy. Mr. Miller stated that Raymond and the Decedent have a judgment of specific performance, that Raymond has obstructed performance, and that he has been sanctioned by Judge Wong and now facing jail time by Judge Wong. Mr. Olsen objected to Mr. Miller's request to have the order amended and his request that half of the rents collected for two and a half years be turned over to Mr. Breen. The Court informed Counsel that there is no petition before it to remove Mr. Breen and appoint Mr. Choy, and that the request regarding rents is not appropriately before the Court. The Petition for Order Instructing Administrator to Convey Title to Real Property is continued to February 7, 2017 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The Court informed the Parties that it will not look favorably on anything that looks like delay, wonders if anything is owed on the property and if there is any return, and suggests that Counsel talk to each other to see where they are.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: L. Maloney. Not reported.

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar Nov-01-2016 Off Calendar

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION, APPOINTMENT UNDER IAEA
Off Calendar Nov-01-2016 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Craig Blackstone. Bailiff: Deputy Rosado. (Not reported)

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar Sep-13-2016 Continued To Nov-01-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Albert Boasberg for petitioner Todd Choy and with Raymond Choy; Maron Miller for John Wei.

The Court noted that Mr. Choy's petition is not procedurally ready, as there had been no response to the probate examiner's notes. Mr. Boasberg apologized, saying that his litigation calendar had been heavy, but that he will be making a response to the notes directly. Mr. Miller argued that the fact Mr. Choy was the decedent's son did not necessarily give him priority for appointment, contending that the statute only gives priority where the family member seeking appointment has an interest in the estate. The Court said that he would be continuing the matter, and that Mr. Miller should save his arguments regarding priority for the hearing. The matter was continued to November 1, 2016 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Craig Blackstone. Bailiff: Deputy Chan. (Not reported)
